codes.summary: Requested validation target is invalid or out of scope.
codes.reason: The requested validation target is absent, malformed, out of scope, or not registered.

recovery.summary: Choose a registered command target, schema, tier, or repository path and rerun.

codes.notes: Preserved from command-surface scaffold and extended with capability/refusal metadata.

codes.reason: The requested validation suite, target path, or artifact reference is not registered or does not exist.

recovery.summary: Choose target all, dominium.validation.run, or the registered contract schema artifact target.

codes.notes: Specific target-not-found refusal for the Workbench validation command slice.

codes.reason: The command input requested a validation target kind that has no registered command adapter.

recovery.summary: Use target_kind validation_suite or contract_schema, or add a reviewed adapter before using another kind.

codes.notes: Prevents broad validation-domain expansion by unregistered target kind.

codes.summary: Validation target is outside the allowed root.
codes.reason: The requested target resolves outside the repository root or outside the allowed roots for the contract_schema slice.

recovery.summary: Choose a target under contracts/command or contracts/schema.

codes.notes: Prevents the Workbench validation projection from becoming an arbitrary filesystem validation surface.

codes.reason: The requested validator or proof command is not available in this repository checkout.

recovery.summary: Install or restore the owning tool, or choose a command whose implementation is registered.

codes.notes: Preserved from command-surface scaffold and extended with capability/refusal metadata.

codes.reason: A required capability was requested but no available capability or provider satisfies it.

recovery.summary: Install, enable, or select a provider/capability that satisfies the request.

codes.notes: Required capability missing must refuse; optional capability missing may degrade explicitly.

codes.reason: A capability is present but does not satisfy the requested version or compatibility range.

recovery.summary: Select a compatible capability/provider or update the requested capability version.

codes.notes: Version negotiation is policy-only in this task.

codes.reason: Two or more requested or selected capabilities are mutually incompatible by declared policy.

recovery.summary: Select a non-conflicting capability set or disable the conflicting feature.

codes.notes: Conflict detection remains future runtime/provider work.

codes.reason: The caller lacks a required declared capability for the command.

recovery.summary: Acquire the required capability through an approved profile, pack, authority, or provider path.

codes.notes: Preserved from command-surface scaffold and made explicit for capability law.

codes.reason: The command is not declared for the requested CLI, TUI, headless, Workbench, server, rendered, test, or AIDE surface.

recovery.summary: Use an allowed surface or update the command registry with proof and compatibility notes.

codes.notes: Preserved from command-surface scaffold.

codes.reason: The command input does not satisfy the declared input schema.

recovery.summary: Validate the input against the command input schema and rerun.

codes.notes: Preserved from command-surface scaffold.

codes.reason: The artifact trust level, provenance, signature, or source review state does not satisfy the requested capability or gate.

recovery.summary: Use a trusted artifact, review and promote provenance, or refuse the operation.

codes.notes: Detailed mod-pack trust remains MOD-PACK-TRUST-MODEL-01.

codes.reason: The schema version is unknown, retired, incompatible, or lacks migration/refusal policy.

recovery.summary: Use a supported schema version or provide an explicit migration/refusal path.

codes.notes: Schema/protocol law owns migration behavior.

codes.reason: No provider is installed, enabled, compatible, or permitted for the requested capability.

recovery.summary: Install, enable, or select a provider that declares the requested capability.

codes.notes: Provider conformance remains PROVIDER-MODEL-01.

codes.reason: The provider ABI does not satisfy the requested public surface, native boundary, or adapter contract.

recovery.summary: Select a provider with a compatible ABI or add an approved adapter.

codes.notes: Native provider ABI remains provisional.

codes.reason: The provider does not declare one or more capabilities required by the selection request.

recovery.summary: Select a provider that declares the required capability or update the descriptor with proof.

codes.notes: Capabilities must reference the governed capability registry.

codes.reason: The provider trust level or provenance does not satisfy the requested operation.

recovery.summary: Select a trusted provider or review/promote provider provenance through governed trust policy.

codes.notes: Detailed trust policy remains later Foundation Lock work.

codes.reason: The provider lacks required conformance tests or fixtures for the requested stability or use.

recovery.summary: Add conformance proof or keep the provider provisional/experimental.

codes.notes: Conformance runner implementation is out of scope for PROVIDER-MODEL-01.

codes.reason: The provider cannot run under the requested OS, deployment target, shell, driver, or platform constraint.

recovery.summary: Select a provider compatible with the current platform or use an explicit degraded mode.

codes.notes: Portability matrix remains later Foundation Lock work.

codes.reason: The current OS, deployment target, driver, shell, or host capability does not satisfy the requested platform requirement.

recovery.summary: Select a supported platform/provider, use degraded mode, or refuse the operation.

codes.notes: Portability matrix remains PORTABILITY-MATRIX-01.

codes.reason: A governing profile, trust policy, security rule, release gate, authority, or capability policy denies the request.

recovery.summary: Inspect the governing policy and evidence; change policy only through reviewed governance.

codes.notes: Security/live-ops policy remains review-gated.

codes.reason: A replacement would affect public, stable, or compatibility-bearing surfaces without conformance, compatibility, or evidence proof.

recovery.summary: Add replacement proof or keep the replacement draft/deferred.

codes.notes: Replacement protocol allows rewrites only with declared proof or explicit deferral.

codes.reason: An active replacement packet lacks rollback action, steps, and verification.

recovery.summary: Add rollback_plan or mark the packet historical/deferred when appropriate.

codes.notes: Rollback is evidence, not runtime implementation in this task.

codes.reason: The replacement affects stable artifacts, schemas, protocols, commands, providers, modules, or public surfaces without a compatible path.

recovery.summary: Provide migration, compatibility proof, or structured refusal before applying the replacement.

codes.notes: Silent compatibility breakage is forbidden.

codes.summary: Replacement requires migration but none is declared.
codes.reason: A replacement would affect a stable or durable data contract without explicit migration or refusal behavior.

recovery.summary: Declare an explicit migration policy or refuse incompatible artifacts deterministically.

codes.notes: No silent or best-effort migration is allowed.

codes.summary: Replacement would break API or ABI obligations.
codes.reason: A replacement affects a public API, ABI, header, symbol, or native boundary without required proof or transition policy.

recovery.summary: Keep the old ABI, add an adapter, or provide reviewed ABI migration/deprecation evidence.

codes.notes: ABI replacement remains provisional until API-ABI-CANON proof exists.

codes.reason: The version is unknown, retired, removed, or outside supported policy.

recovery.summary: Use a supported version, run a declared migration, or select a supported replacement surface.

codes.notes: Unsupported versions must refuse with structured evidence.

codes.reason: The version does not satisfy the declared compatibility range.

recovery.summary: Select a compatible version or replacement, or use a declared migration path.

codes.notes: Compatibility is declared policy, not semver inference.

codes.reason: The surface is no longer active for new use and must use replacement, migration, compatibility bridge, or refusal policy.

recovery.summary: Use the replacement surface, migrate through the declared path, or inspect retirement evidence.

codes.notes: Retirement is not deletion; old references need policy.

codes.reason: The surface is no longer available and no active compatibility bridge is selected.

recovery.summary: Inspect removal evidence and use the declared replacement or migration path when available.

codes.notes: Removed old references must refuse deterministically or bridge through declared compatibility.

codes.reason: The requested version or surface cannot proceed without an explicit migration policy.

recovery.summary: Run the declared migration or refuse until a reviewed migration policy exists.

codes.notes: No best-effort migration is permitted.

codes.reason: A deprecated, retired, removed, or breaking surface needs a declared replacement or reason no replacement exists.

recovery.summary: Declare or select the replacement surface, or record why no replacement exists with refusal policy.

codes.notes: Nothing deprecated disappears without replacement, migration, or explicit refusal policy.

codes.summary: Mod or pack trust is insufficient.
codes.reason: The requested mod, pack, module, adapter, or provider authority exceeds its declared trust level or review status.

recovery.summary: Inspect trust evidence, lower requested authority, or complete review before enabling.

codes.notes: Trust cannot be inferred from pack existence.

codes.reason: A mod or pack requested a permission without a declared scope, reason, and trust policy.

recovery.summary: Remove the permission request or declare and review it before enabling the feature.

codes.notes: A capability is not a permission.

codes.reason: Native code was requested outside trusted/signed native provider policy and conformance evidence.

recovery.summary: Use a non-native provider or keep the provider inside a trusted build boundary with conformance evidence.

codes.notes: Dynamic native loading remains forbidden by default.

codes.reason: An external process adapter lacks declared protocol, permissions, review, sandbox, security, or determinism evidence.

recovery.summary: Disable the adapter or declare IPC/protocol, permissions, determinism impact, and review evidence.

codes.notes: External adapters are not invisible fallback.

codes.reason: A mod, pack, adapter, or provider omits determinism impact or replay policy.

recovery.summary: Declare determinism classification, replay policy, and authoritative-state impact.

codes.notes: Unknown determinism impact refuses by default.

codes.reason: A pack overlay conflicts with another pack or target without an allowed explicit conflict policy.

recovery.summary: Declare explicit overlay conflict policy or remove one of the conflicting overlays.

codes.notes: Runtime mount plans are future work; policy is declared here.

codes.summary: Silent pack overlay overwrite is forbidden.
codes.reason: A pack overlay attempts implicit or silent overwrite behavior.

recovery.summary: Use an explicit conflict policy with evidence or refuse the pack composition.

codes.notes: No silent overwrite.

codes.reason: A mod or pack requests network authority without an allowed trust level and declaration.

recovery.summary: Remove the network permission request or isolate it behind reviewed external adapter policy.

codes.notes: Data-only packs get no network authority.

codes.reason: A mod or pack requests filesystem authority without an allowed trust level and declaration.

recovery.summary: Remove the filesystem permission request or bind it to governed storage/profile/save policy.

codes.notes: Data-only packs get no filesystem authority beyond own declared pack payload.

codes.reason: The requested OS/platform floor is unregistered, unsupported, or lacks the required proof level.

recovery.summary: Select a registered platform floor with evidence, or keep the target planned/research.

codes.notes: Support is a matrix row plus evidence, not a folder or preset.

codes.reason: The requested CPU architecture is not registered or lacks portability evidence.

recovery.summary: Select a registered architecture with proof or record the target as research.

codes.notes: Endianness and word size assumptions must be declared.

codes.reason: The requested compiler/toolchain is unknown, unsupported, or lacks build proof.

recovery.summary: Select a registered proven toolchain, add evidence, or lower the claim to planned/research.

codes.notes: Installed compiler presence is not support proof.

codes.summary: Required provider is unavailable on the target.
codes.reason: A runtime, renderer, storage, network, audio, input, or package provider is not available or not proven for the requested target.

recovery.summary: Select an available provider, use explicit degraded mode, or refuse the capability.

codes.notes: Provider fallback must be explicit typed degradation.

codes.reason: The requested CLI, headless, rendered, native, or package product mode lacks runtime/provider capability or proof on the target.

recovery.summary: Choose a product mode with runtime capability evidence, or add proof before claiming support.

codes.notes: App folders do not prove product mode support.

codes.reason: The requested release or package support claim lacks build, smoke, product, package, or release evidence.

recovery.summary: Run the required gate or lower the support_status/release_status before publishing the claim.

codes.notes: Release support requires release gate evidence.

codes.reason: The target requested source_native_64 while using a 32-bit architecture.

recovery.summary: Use source_native_64 on x86_64/arm64 or reclassify the target as constrained_native_32, contract_projection, or archive_runner.

codes.notes: Added by PORTABILITY-ARCH-POLICY-02.

codes.reason: A stable format attempted to use native pointer-width, raw pointer, native padding, or compiler object layout data.

recovery.summary: Replace native-width fields with fixed-width explicit encodings and provide migration/refusal behavior.

codes.notes: Added by PORTABILITY-ARCH-POLICY-02.

codes.reason: The target requested unsupported endian behavior or omitted explicit little-endian persisted/protocol encoding.

recovery.summary: Use little-endian mainline encoding or a reviewed projection/research lane.

codes.notes: Added by PORTABILITY-ARCH-POLICY-02.

codes.reason: The architecture claim lacks required build, smoke, product, package, release, or capability evidence.

recovery.summary: Add the required proof or lower the support status before claiming support.

codes.notes: Added by PORTABILITY-ARCH-POLICY-02.

codes.summary: Legacy target requires projection or constrained mode.
codes.reason: The requested vintage or 32-bit target is outside source_native_64 mainline product policy.

recovery.summary: Use constrained-native, contract projection, replay/snapshot client, or archive-runner behavior.

codes.notes: Added by PORTABILITY-ARCH-POLICY-02.

codes.reason: The composition plan is malformed, unsupported, unordered, or missing required policy/evidence fields.

recovery.summary: Inspect the composition validator findings, repair the plan, and rerun validation.

codes.notes: Composition resolver contract refusal; no runtime resolver is implemented.

codes.reason: A composition fixture references an app, pack, module, provider, capability, refusal, diagnostic, or profile ID absent from the governing registries.

recovery.summary: Select a registered governed ID or add a reviewed registry entry before composing.

codes.notes: Prevents path or convenience references from becoming composition identity.

codes.summary: Silent composition overlay overwrite is forbidden.
codes.reason: A pack, profile, or workspace overlay attempts to overwrite an overlapping contribution without explicit conflict policy and evidence.

recovery.summary: Declare explicit conflict behavior with evidence, select a different overlay, or refuse the composition.

codes.notes: Extends pack overlay law to product composition lock/report surfaces.

codes.reason: A selected, degraded, refused, or locked composition lacks required diagnostics, fallback trace, conflict report, or evidence reference.

recovery.summary: Produce the required evidence packet, fallback trace, diagnostic, or refusal report.

codes.notes: Evidence is required before any composition result can be used as proof.

codes.reason: A provider ID was absent from the provider registry.

recovery.summary: Use a registered provider ID or add a reviewed provider descriptor.

codes.notes: Provider IDs are governed identifiers, not paths.

codes.reason: A service ID was absent from the governed service registry.

recovery.summary: Use a registered service ID or add a reviewed service descriptor.

codes.notes: Paths, command IDs, and provider IDs are not service identity.

codes.reason: The service descriptor or conformance status is planned or fixture-only.

recovery.summary: Select an implemented service with passing conformance or keep the request planned/fixture-only.

codes.notes: Planned and fixture-only conformance does not imply support.

codes.reason: The service lacks a passing conformance suite required for the requested stability or support claim.

recovery.summary: Add passing conformance evidence or lower the service/support claim.

codes.notes: Stable service claims require passing conformance.

codes.reason: The selected service or provider does not declare required capabilities.

recovery.summary: Select a matching service/provider, explicitly degrade, or refuse.

codes.notes: Capability selection must emit selected, degraded, refused, or evidence decisions.

codes.reason: A provider declined a service-backed request under declared limits.

recovery.summary: Select another provider, use explicit degraded behavior, or keep the service request refused.

codes.notes: Provider refusal is typed lawful result.

codes.reason: The replay/proof input fixture is malformed, references missing material, or fails the command input binding.

recovery.summary: Inspect replay proof validator output, repair the input fixture, and rerun validation.

codes.notes: Command-level replay/proof only; no runtime replay engine is implemented.

codes.summary: Replay proof expected hash is missing.
codes.reason: A replay manifest, proof manifest, proof hash, or verification result lacks the required canonical sha256 expected hash.

recovery.summary: Regenerate the canonical hash from sorted UTF-8 JSON material and update the fixture.

codes.notes: Hashes are over canonical serialization, not native object layout.

codes.reason: Observed canonical replay/proof material does not match the expected hash.

recovery.summary: Inspect the proof material refs and either repair the fixture or preserve the typed mismatch refusal.

codes.notes: Replay mismatch must refuse; silent acceptance is forbidden.

codes.summary: Replay proof references an unknown command.
codes.reason: The replay manifest or proof material references a command absent from the command surface registry.

recovery.summary: Use a registered command ID or add a reviewed command surface before creating replay proof.

codes.notes: Command identity is registry-backed and semantic, not a path.

codes.reason: A replay/proof fixture claims world, save, gameplay, product, or runtime replay support outside the command-level fixture scope.

recovery.summary: Keep target_scope as command_result_fixture or defer broader replay work to a reviewed runtime task.

codes.notes: Full game/world/save replay is explicitly not implemented by this slice.

codes.reason: A replay/proof manifest references missing evidence or omits required evidence refs.

recovery.summary: Add the missing evidence reference or keep the replay proof refused.

codes.notes: Replay/proof material is derived evidence, not source truth.

codes.reason: Replay/proof material uses native object layout, path identity, or an unsupported serialization policy.

recovery.summary: Use the declared sorted UTF-8 canonical JSON policy for this fixture-level proof.

codes.notes: Native object layout and paths are not replay/proof identity.

codes.summary: Fixture-only replay proof cannot claim runtime support.
codes.reason: A replay/proof fixture claims runtime replay, package runtime, world replay, save replay, gameplay replay, or release support.

recovery.summary: Keep the proof as fixture-only evidence or defer runtime support to a reviewed implementation task.

codes.notes: Fixture proof is evidence for a narrow command path only.

codes.reason: The barebones client slice provides CLI/headless status only and does not implement renderer or rendered UI support.

recovery.summary: Use CLI/headless status, diag, or verify surfaces until a reviewed rendered client shell task lands.

codes.notes: Rendered/native UI remains blocked outside this task.

codes.reason: The barebones client slice does not implement playable gameplay or domain runtime behavior.

recovery.summary: Use status, diag, or verify surfaces and defer gameplay to a reviewed product/runtime task.

codes.notes: Barebones proof must not claim playable gameplay.

codes.reason: World and save runtime support is outside the barebones client proof.

recovery.summary: Use barebones status, diag, or verify; defer world/save runtime to reviewed runtime work.

codes.notes: World/save runtime support is not implemented.

codes.reason: Package mount is fixture/proof-level only and the client does not implement runtime package mounting.

recovery.summary: Use barebones status, diag, or verify; defer package runtime to reviewed package runtime work.

codes.notes: Package runtime and mod loading remain blocked.

codes.reason: Provider runtime selection/loading is outside the barebones client proof.

recovery.summary: Use barebones status, diag, or verify; defer provider runtime to reviewed provider work.

codes.notes: Provider runtime remains blocked.

codes.summary: Client runtime module loader is unavailable.
codes.reason: Runtime module loading is outside the barebones client proof.

recovery.summary: Use barebones status, diag, or verify; defer runtime module loading to reviewed module/runtime work.

codes.notes: Runtime module loading remains blocked.

codes.reason: The requested client mode is outside the barebones CLI/headless status proof surface.

recovery.summary: Choose status, diag, verify, CLI, or headless mode until a reviewed task adds broader support.

codes.notes: Unsupported client modes refuse explicitly instead of silently falling back or claiming runtime support.

notes: Capability/refusal law extends the command-level refusal scaffold; MOD-PACK-TRUST-MODEL-01 adds mod/pack trust refusal codes without implementing runtime resolution.
